systems.title: Entering the Classroom
systems.objective: Students will enter the classroom calmly, unpack materials, and begin morning work independently.
systems.iDo: Model entering the classroom exactly the way students should do it. Walk in, unpack, turn in the folder, get materials, sit down, and begin morning work. Then model it incorrectly and ask students what went wrong.
systems.weDo: Have the whole class practice entering together. Narrate the positive choices you see: quiet feet, folders turned in, eyes on morning work.
systems.youDo: Send students outside or to the doorway in small groups and have them practice independently while you observe.
teacherLanguage: Show me how scholars enter calmly.
Try that again with quiet feet.
I notice students getting started without reminders.
This is how we protect our learning time.
commonMistakes: Waiting for the teacher instead of starting
reteachMoments: Any morning that begins loudly or scattered
systems.micaylaNote: Do not assume students know how to enter. This routine sets the tone for the entire day. Practice it until it feels automatic.

systems.title: Morning Routine
systems.objective: Students will follow a predictable morning routine so the school day begins smoothly.
systems.iDo: Walk students through the entire routine step by step. Point to each part of the visual routine as you model.
systems.weDo: Practice as a class using a call-and-response: What do we do first? What comes next?
systems.youDo: Students complete the routine while you silently observe and note who needs support.
teacherLanguage: Your morning routine is your first success of the day.
Check the chart if you forget.
I love how you started without waiting for me.
Our room feels peaceful when we all follow the routine.
reteachMoments: When students begin relying on teacher reminders
systems.micaylaNote: This routine prevents morning chaos. The more consistent you are here, the easier the rest of the day becomes.

systems.title: How to Line Up
systems.objective: Students will line up safely, quietly, and quickly when called.
systems.iDo: Model each step slowly. Then model a messy line-up and let students name what needs fixing. Model correctly one more time.
systems.weDo: Call a small group to line up while the rest of the class notices what they did well. Repeat with another group.
systems.youDo: Have the whole class line up. If it is not correct, calmly reset and try again.
teacherLanguage: Line up like scholars.
Reset. We can do this better.
I see hands to self and voices off.
A safe line helps us move through the school respectfully.
systems.micaylaNote: Spend time here. If students line up well, transitions become much easier all year.

systems.title: Hallway Expectations
systems.objective: Students will move through the hallway safely and respectfully.
systems.iDo: Model walking in the hallway with silent voice, hands by side, and eyes forward. Show what not to do and let students identify the problem.
systems.weDo: Practice walking to a nearby checkpoint and back. Stop and praise what is correct.
systems.youDo: Walk to lunch, recess, or specials using the expectations. Reset if needed.
teacherLanguage: The hallway belongs to everyone.
Silent voices show respect.
Let’s return and try again.
Our class can move calmly.
systems.micaylaNote: It is okay to stop, return, and practice again. That is teaching, not punishment.

systems.title: How to Sit on the Carpet
systems.objective: Students will sit on the carpet in a way that shows they are ready to learn.
materials: Read aloud or mini lesson materials
systems.iDo: Model ready carpet body. Then model a not-ready body and ask students to explain the difference.
systems.weDo: Students practice moving from desks to carpet and showing ready body. Praise specific behaviors.
systems.youDo: During read aloud or mini lesson, students maintain ready body with quick teacher reminders.
teacherLanguage: Show me a ready carpet body.
Bubble in mouth.
Eyes on the speaker.
Your body is showing me you are ready.
systems.micaylaNote: This routine will need quick refreshers often. Keep the language simple and consistent.

systems.title: Pencil Procedures
systems.objective: Students will manage pencils without interrupting instruction.
systems.iDo: Show students exactly what to do with a dull pencil, broken pencil, and missing pencil.
systems.weDo: Practice scenarios: My pencil breaks during writing. What do I do? I do not have a pencil. What do I do?
systems.youDo: Students use the pencil system during independent work without asking aloud.
teacherLanguage: Use the pencil system.
This is a tool, not a toy.
Solve the pencil problem quietly.
Thank you for returning borrowed pencils.
commonMistakes: Interrupting to ask for a pencil
systems.micaylaNote: Pencils can steal instructional time. A clear system saves your sanity.

systems.title: Restroom Signal
systems.objective: Students will request and use the restroom without interrupting learning.
systems.iDo: Model the hand signal and quiet permission process. Show how to leave and rejoin without interrupting.
systems.weDo: Practice the signal as a class. Role-play what to do if the teacher is speaking.
systems.youDo: Students use the signal during the day and wait for a nonverbal response when possible.
teacherLanguage: Use the signal.
Thank you for waiting.
Return quietly and join us.
Take care of your body and protect learning time.
reteachMoments: When the pass system breaks down
systems.micaylaNote: Restroom signals reduce interruptions and help the room stay calm.

systems.title: Partner Talk
systems.objective: Students will use partner talk to rehearse ideas before sharing with the class.
systems.iDo: Model partner talk with a student or imaginary partner. Show listening and speaking roles.
systems.weDo: Practice with a simple question before using academic content.
systems.youDo: Students use partner talk during reading, vocabulary, writing, and math discussions.
teacherLanguage: Partner A speaks first.
Partner B listens.
Now switch.
Be ready to share your partner’s idea.
systems.micaylaNote: Assign Partner A and Partner B clearly. Otherwise students waste time deciding.

systems.title: Voice Levels
systems.objective: Students will use appropriate voice levels for different learning activities.
systems.iDo: Model each voice level. Exaggerate the differences so students can hear them clearly.
systems.weDo: Call out a voice level and have students practice with a short phrase.
systems.youDo: Students use the correct voice level during centers, partner work, independent work, and presentations.
teacherLanguage: Show me Level 1.
We are at Level 0 right now.
Partner work is Level 2.
Match your voice to the activity.
systems.micaylaNote: Voice levels only work if students practice them and you refer to them constantly.

systems.title: Dismissal Routine
systems.objective: Students will pack up and dismiss safely and calmly.
systems.iDo: Model packing up, cleaning, and waiting quietly. Show what rushing looks like and why it is unsafe.
systems.weDo: Practice packing slowly with teacher directions.
systems.youDo: Students pack independently and wait for dismissal signal.
teacherLanguage: Dismissal is still part of our learning day.
Pack calmly.
Check your area.
Wait until your group is called.
systems.micaylaNote: Start dismissal earlier than you think during the first week. Calm dismissal is taught.
